sourceMovie.title: Millennium Actress
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Millennium Actress (2002) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wind Rises
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Millennium Actress clicked for you, The Wind Rises (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and animation, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Millennium Actress?
faq.answer: Try The Wind Rises, The Boy and the Heron, The Tale of The Princess Kaguya—each shares DNA with Millennium Actress while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Millennium Actress?
faq.answer: Millennium Actress (2002) is best described as Drama, Animation, Romance, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Millennium Actress worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
